description: Multi-epoch-span Delta (protocol.md § Multi-epoch-span delta, #lzsync). A Delta MAY advance more than one epoch in a single frame (epoch > base_epoch + 1) when the producer's epoch is a cumulative accepted-event count that coalesces several events into one flush. Normative: applying one Delta { base_epoch, epoch, ops } equals applying the same ops in order as a run of unit deltas advancing last_epoch from base_epoch to epoch (batch = fold, proven ReliableSync.multi_epoch_apply_eq_fold); last_epoch advances atomically to epoch only after the whole op list applies; the gap rule (base_epoch == last_epoch) is unchanged. The span-1 case (epoch == base_epoch + 1) is the ordinary Delta, so every existing Delta fixture stays valid. The `wire` frame validates against schemas/delta.json.

scenarios.name: span_3_applies_equal_to_unit_fold
scenarios.description: A receiver at last_epoch=40 applying this one span-3 delta reaches the same graph and the same last_epoch=43 as a receiver that applied three unit deltas (40->41, 41->42, 42->43) carrying the same ops in order.

scenarios.name: gap_rule_unchanged_under_span
scenarios.description: A span-3 delta whose base_epoch != last_epoch is still a gap; the span does not relax gap detection.
